pattern: Reconstructing every legal IPv4 address from a digit string is a bounded backtracking that places exactly four segments, each a 1-to-3 digit value in range with no superfluous leading zero.
hints: A valid address is exactly four parts separated by dots, so the search places four segments and must consume the whole string.
Each segment is one to three digits, is at most 255, and may be a lone zero but never a multi-digit number starting with zero.

Backtrack over the cut after each segment, pruning when remaining digits cannot fill the rest within one-to-three per part, and record the dotted join once four valid segments exactly exhaust the string.

edge_inputs.description: all zeros must split into single zeros
too short to ever form four parts
too long to fit in four parts of at most three digits
leading zeros forbid a multi-digit zero-prefixed part
twelve digits with values forcing the 255 cap

stress.description: random short digit strings within the four-part feasible length window
stress.note: length 4..12 keeps strings inside the range that can plausibly form addresses
